title: Montreal 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Montreal 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Montreal, Canada: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Notre-Dame Basilica of Montreal and Montreal Biodome. Prices from $1796 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Montreal, Canada — Humid continental climate with cold, snowy winters (highs often below 0°C, Dec-Feb) and warm, humid summers (highs around 26-27°C, Jun-Aug). June to September brings warm days, packed festival calendars and lively terrasses; February-March suits winter and Igloofest lovers who dress for the cold. This itinerary blends Notre-Dame Basilica of Montreal, Montreal Biodome, Old Montreal (Vieux-Montréal) streets with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Notre-Dame Basilica of Montreal, Montreal Biodome, Old Montreal (Vieux-Montréal) streets
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Montreal
itinerary.description: Land at Montréal-Trudeau International Airport (YUL) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Notre-Dame Basilica of Montreal — Gothic Revival basilica with a dazzling blue-and-gold interior. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Montreal: Montreal Biodome & Montreal Botanical Garden
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Montreal Biodome — Indoor ecosystems recreating four distinct environments. In the afternoon, continue to Montreal Botanical Garden — One of the world's great botanical gardens including a Chinese and Japanese garden. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Montreal: Old Port of Montreal attractions & Musée des beaux-arts de Montréal (MMFA)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Old Port of Montreal attractions — Zipline, Ferris wheel (La Grande Roue) and boat tours along the waterfront. In the afternoon, continue to Musée des beaux-arts de Montréal (MMFA) — Major fine arts museum, permanent collection free, special exhibits ticketed. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Quebec City
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Quebec City, a popular side trip from Montreal. Return to Montreal in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Marché Bonsecours dome hall — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Montreal: Pointe-à-Callière Museum & Mount Royal lookout and Chalet
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Pointe-à-Callière Museum — Archaeology and history museum built over the city's birthplace. In the afternoon, continue to Mount Royal lookout and Chalet — Panoramic city viewpoint atop the mountain the city is named for. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Laurentian Mountains (Mont-Tremblant)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Laurentian Mountains (Mont-Tremblant), a popular side trip from Montreal. Return to Montreal in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Montreal: Montreal Science Centre & Insectarium de Montréal
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Montreal Science Centre — Interactive science exhibits and an IMAX theater at the Old Port. In the afternoon, continue to Insectarium de Montréal — Renovated immersive insect museum in the Space for Life complex. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Habitat 67 exterior viewing — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Ottawa
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Ottawa, a popular side trip from Montreal. Return to Montreal in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Montreal: Rio Tinto Alcan Planetarium & La Ronde amusement park
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Rio Tinto Alcan Planetarium — Modern planetarium with immersive dome shows. In the afternoon, continue to La Ronde amusement park — Six Flags-affiliated amusement park on Île Sainte-Hélène. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Montreal: Old Montreal (Vieux-Montréal) streets & Mount Royal Park
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Old Montreal (Vieux-Montréal) streets — Cobblestone 17th-18th century district free to wander. In the afternoon, continue to Mount Royal Park — Frederick Law Olmsted-designed park with the city's best skyline lookout. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Parc Jean-Drapeau trails — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Rue Saint-Paul or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to YUL for your departure flight.

travelTips: Try smoked meat at Schwartz's or poutine at La Banquise — both are genuine local institutions, not tourist traps.
Book Jazz Fest or Just for Laughs accommodations early — downtown hotels fill up fast in summer.
The Underground City is a great way to avoid winter cold while still reaching shops, the Metro and Bell Centre.
Bixi bike-share is a cheap, fun way to cover the Plateau and Old Montreal in summer.
Most service staff switch fluently to English if greeted first in French — it's a courtesy, not a requirement.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
A heavy winter coat and insulated boots if visiting December-March
Comfortable shoes for cobblestone streets in Old Montreal
Light layers for humid summer days
A few basic French phrases go a long way with locals
thingsToAvoid: Underestimating deep winter cold — wind chill regularly pushes temperatures well below what the forecast shows.
Assuming everything is bilingual — some smaller Francophone establishments operate mostly in French.
Driving in Old Montreal — narrow one-way cobblestone streets and limited parking make walking easier.
Skipping reservations at Joe Beef or Toqué! — both book out weeks ahead.
Missing the last Metro (around 1am) and getting stranded without a rideshare plan.
topRestaurants: Au Pied de Cochon — Quebecois Comfort Food ($$$)
Le Vin Papillon — Wine Bar/Small Plates ($$$)
instagramSpots: Old Port waterfront with La Grande Roue
hiddenGems: Ruelles vertes (green laneways) of the Plateau
familyActivities: Old Port Ferris wheel and zipline
adventureActivities: Ice skating on Mount Royal's Beaver Lake in winter
luxuryExperiences: Private Old Montreal horse-drawn carriage ride
Suite stay at Hôtel Le St-James
Private wine cellar dinner in Old Montreal
honeymoonExperiences: Romantic dinner at Le Vin Papillon
Horse-drawn calèche ride through Old Montreal
Winter ice-skating date at Beaver Lake
budgetExperiences: Free skyline view from the Mount Royal Chalet
Smoked meat sandwich at Schwartz's for a few dollars
Wander Old Montreal and Rue Saint-Paul
Free summer outdoor concerts in the Quartier des Spectacles

festivalCalendar.name: Igloofest
festivalCalendar.timing: January, outdoor winter electronic music festival

localEtiquette: Starting conversations with "Bonjour" (or "Bonjour/Hi") is appreciated even if the rest continues in English.
Tipping 15-18% at restaurants and bars is standard, similar to the rest of Canada.
Quebec French has its own vocabulary and accent distinct from France — locals appreciate visitors attempting French phrases.
Terrasse (patio) culture is central to summer social life — expect lively outdoor dining until late.
Winter requires genuine cold-weather etiquette: stamping snow off boots indoors, dressing warmly for outdoor waits.
localTransportGuide: The STM Metro is efficient, colorful and covers the island well, complemented by an extensive bus network and the BIXI bike-share in warmer months. The Underground City (RÉSO) lets you walk between many downtown buildings, shops and Metro stations without going outside — handy in winter.
bestTimeToVisit: June to September brings warm days, packed festival calendars and lively terrasses; February-March suits winter and Igloofest lovers who dress for the cold.
weather: Humid continental climate with cold, snowy winters (highs often below 0°C, Dec-Feb) and warm, humid summers (highs around 26-27°C, Jun-Aug).
visaInformation: Visa-exempt eTA (Electronic Travel Authorization) for UK, EU, Australian and other eligible passport holders arriving by air for short stays. Indian nationals and most other nationalities require a Canadian visitor visa (TRV) arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~13.6h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Montreal?
faqs.answer: Visa-exempt eTA (Electronic Travel Authorization) for UK, EU, Australian and other eligible passport holders arriving by air for short stays. Indian nationals and most other nationalities require a Canadian visitor visa (TRV) arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Montreal?
faqs.answer: June to September brings warm days, packed festival calendars and lively terrasses; February-March suits winter and Igloofest lovers who dress for the cold. Humid continental climate with cold, snowy winters (highs often below 0°C, Dec-Feb) and warm, humid summers (highs around 26-27°C, Jun-Aug).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Montreal safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Montreal is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Montreal?
faqs.answer: The local currency is CAD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Mia Wang
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Underground City (RÉSO) and Musée des beaux-arts de Montréal (MMFA). Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Omar Dubois
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Mount Royal lookout and Chalet and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Ava Johnson
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Lachine Canal bike path alone was worth the trip, but combined with Jean-Talon Market this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: James Garcia
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Our trip to Montreal was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Plateau Mont-Royal streets was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Ava Johnson
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Old Port of Montreal attractions alone was worth the trip, but combined with Pointe-à-Callière Museum this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: James Andersen
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Our trip to Montreal was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Notre-Dame Basilica exterior was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Nadia van Dijk
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Montreal is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Plateau Mont-Royal streets. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.
